source.title: Hippocampal neurons encode information about different types of memory episodes occurring in the same location

confidence_rationale: Foundational splitter-cell paper. Two-thirds of CA1 cells fire differentially at the same place depending on trial-type context, after controlling for position/heading/speed. Strong direct support for (place, context) anchor encoding rather than pure place-keyed anchors.
failure_signatures: Pure place-keyed anchors will collapse two distinct (place, context) attractors into one and lose the disambiguation biology preserves.

One-third of cells fired similarly on both trial types (pure place coding) -- the substrate must accommodate a mixture of pure-place and (place, context) anchors, not assume all anchors carry context.

mapping.source_claim_statement: On a continuous spatial alternation T-maze, two-thirds of CA1 complex-spike neurons fire differentially as rats traverse the central stem on left-turn vs right-turn trials, after controlling for position, heading, and running speed.
mapping.ree_translation: Anchors must be (place, stream-mixture) pairs rather than place alone. The per-stream V_s vector in the substrate plan is a candidate disambiguator playing the role that 'trial type' plays here. MECH-272 routing then selects the (place, stream-mixture) attractor that matches current input.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The biology disambiguator may be prospective trajectory, retrospective trajectory, or abstract trial type -- subsequent work parses these differently. The substrate's stream-mixture vector is one possible mapping but not uniquely supported. Stereotyped alternation may inflate the splitter signal vs naturalistic contexts.
mapping.source_context: Single-unit CA1 recordings in rats running continuous spatial alternation on modified T-maze.

confidence_components.notes: High-quality foundational paper. Mapping fidelity strong for the qualitative (place, context) claim; the specific stream-mixture mapping in REE is one of several biologically plausible disambiguators.
